subject: UX & Research
title: The Autofill Disaster
description: A quick, practical guide to The Autofill Disaster.

hook.text: A user buys a gift online. Autofill enters their home address. The gift ships to themselves, not the recipient.

buildup.text: Browser autofill matches field names and fills everything instantly. But 'shipping address' gets the saved home address.

discovery.text: Studies show 30% of gift purchases have address errors from autofill pulling the wrong saved data.

twist.text: Amazon asks 'Is this a gift?' early in the flow. That single question disables autofill for the address section.

climax.text: Autofill helps 90% of the time. But for the 10% it fails, clear warnings and confirmation prevent costly errors.

punchline.text: Autofill helps most users but trips up the rest. Plan for both.

quiz.question: How do some sites prevent autofill from entering the wrong shipping address for gifts?
options: Asking 'Is this a gift?' early to disable autofill for the address section
Disabling autofill entirely for all users
Requiring users to type addresses twice
